key_insight: LLM-ACES turns ODE discovery into a closed-loop process: an LLM proposes symbolic operator priors, and the disagreement among candidate equations drives targeted new data collection to resolve identifiability gaps.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable system identification from sparse or noisy trajectories, which is valuable for scientific modeling, control, and digital-twin applications where data acquisition is expensive.
method: LLM partitions the symbolic search space with operator priors.
Candidate equations are fit within each region to the observed trajectories.
Active acquisition selects informative new trajectories based on candidate disagreement.
evidence_signal: Across 122 ODE systems from ODEBench and ODEBase, the method reports the lowest median NMSE, 46.2% and 52.4% symbolic accuracy, and strong gains with only one-tenth the data.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of LLM-generated priors and the availability of a closed-loop data acquisition mechanism, so it may be harder to deploy when new trajectories cannot be actively sampled.
paper_title: LLM-ACES: Closed-Loop Discovery of Dynamical Systems with LLM-Guided Adaptive Search
